title: Laboratory Biosafety Guidelines for working with SARS-CoV-2 | COVID-19 | CDC
text:
) issued guidelines specifically for SARS-CoV-2. This revised guidance is specific to SARS-CoV-2 and should not be extrapolated to other members of the Coronaviridae family or to other members of the species Betacoronavirus pandemicum . 
Laboratories working with novel or emerging coronaviruses, such as those transmitted from animals to humans (e.g. new zoonotic coronaviruses), must evaluate the varying degrees of risk associated with coronaviruses. They should perform detailed risk assessments that take into account the specific location, the activities conducted, and the particular coronaviruses involved. 
Summary of recent changes

Specified pathogens covered by the guidance 
View previous updates .
Key points 
This guidance is intended for laboratory and support staff who handle or process SARS-CoV-2 samples such as clinical specimens, cultures, and environmental samples. 
All laboratories should perform a site-specific and activity-specific comprehensive risk assessment in collaboration with biosafety professionals, laboratory management, and other scientific and safety experts, and follow Standard Precautions when handling clinical specimens. See clinical specimen and laboratory safety guidance .
